info.name: Stripe Customers API
Customers
Stripe Get   Customers

params.name: email
params.description: A case-sensitive filter on the list based on the customer's `email` field. The value must be a string.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

params.name: test_clock
params.description: Provides a list of customers that are associated with the specified test clock. The response will not include customers with test clocks if this parameter is not set.

items.docs: Returns a list of your customers. The customers are returned sorted by creation date, with the most recent customers appearing first.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers

items.docs: Creates a new customer object.

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Search

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: page
params.description: A cursor for pagination across multiple pages of results. Don't include this parameter on the first call. Use the next_page value returned in a previous response to request subsequent results.

params.name: query
params.description: The search query string. See [search query language](https://stripe.com/docs/search#search-query-language) and the list of supported [query fields for customers](https://stripe.com/docs/search#query-fields-for-customers).

items.docs: Search for customers you’ve previously created using Stripe’s Search Query Language. Don’t use search in read-after-write flows where strict consistency is necessary. Under normal operating conditions, data is searchable in less than a minute. Occasionally, propagation of new or updated data can be up to an hour behind during outages. Search functionality is not available to merchants in India.

info.name: Customer
Stripe Get   Customers Customer

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves a Customer object.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer

items.docs: Updates the specified customer by setting the values of the parameters passed. Any parameters not provided will be left unchanged. For example, if you pass the source parameter, that becomes the customer’s active source (e.g., a card) to be used for all charges in the future. When you update a customer to a new valid card source by passing the source parameter: for each of the customer’s current subscriptions, if the subscription bills automatically and is in

info.name: Stripe Delete   Customers Customer

items.docs: Permanently deletes a customer. It cannot be undone. Also immediately cancels any active subscriptions on the customer.

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Discount

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Discount

info.name: Stripe Delete   Customers Customer Discount

items.docs: Removes the currently applied discount on a customer.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Funding Instructions

items.docs: Retrieve funding instructions for a customer cash balance. If funding instructions do not yet exist for the customer, new funding instructions will be created. If funding instructions have already been created for a given customer, the same funding instructions will be retrieved. In other words, we will return the same funding instructions each time.

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Payment Methods

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

params.name: type
params.description: An optional filter on the list, based on the object `type` field. Without the filter, the list includes all current and future payment method types. If your integration expects only one type of payment method in the response, make sure to provide a type value in the request.

items.docs: Returns a list of PaymentMethods for a given Customer

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Payment Methods Payment Method

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves a PaymentMethod object for a given Customer.

For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: object
params.description: Filter sources according to a particular object type.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

items.docs: List sources for a specified customer.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Sources

items.docs:
When you create a new credit card, you must specify a customer or recipient on which to create it.

If the card’s owner has no default card, then the new card will become the default. However, if the owner already has a default, then it will not change. To change the default, you should update the customer to have a new default_source.

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Sources Id

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieve a specified source for a given customer.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Sources Id

items.docs: Update a specified source for a given customer.

info.name: Stripe Delete   Customers Customer Sources Id

items.docs: Delete a specified source for a given customer.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Sources Id Verify

items.docs: Verify a specified bank account for a given customer.

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Subscriptions

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

items.docs: You can see a list of the customer’s active subscriptions. Note that the 10 most recent active subscriptions are always available by default on the customer object. If you need more than those 10, you can use the limit and starting_after parameters to page through additional subscriptions.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Subscriptions

items.docs: Creates a new subscription on an existing customer.

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Subscriptions Subscription Exposed Id

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves the subscription with the given ID.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Subscriptions Subscription Exposed Id

items.docs: Updates an existing subscription on a customer to match the specified parameters. When changing plans or quantities, we will optionally prorate the price we charge next month to make up for any price changes. To preview how the proration will be calculated, use the upcoming invoice endpoint.

info.name: Stripe Delete   Customers Customer Subscriptions Subscription Exposed Id

items.docs:
Cancels a customer’s subscription. If you set the at_period_end parameter to true, the subscription will remain active until the end of the period, at which point it will be canceled and not renewed. Otherwise, with the default false value, the subscription is terminated immediately. In either case, the customer will not be charged again for the subscription.

Note, however, that any pending invoice items that you’ve created will still be charged

params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Most credit balance transaction fields are immutable, but you may update its description and metadata.

info.name: Accounts
Stripe Get   Customers Customer Bank Accounts

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

items.docs: You can see a list of the bank accounts belonging to a Customer. Note that the 10 most recent sources are always available by default on the Customer. If you need more than those 10, you can use this API method and the limit and starting_after parameters to page through additional bank accounts.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Bank Accounts

items.docs:
When you create a new credit card, you must specify a customer or recipient on which to create it.

If the card’s owner has no default card, then the new card will become the default. However, if the owner already has a default, then it will not change. To change the default, you should update the customer to have a new default_source.

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Bank Accounts Id

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: By default, you can see the 10 most recent sources stored on a Customer directly on the object, but you can also retrieve details about a specific bank account stored on the Stripe account.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Bank Accounts Id

items.docs: Update a specified source for a given customer.

info.name: Stripe Delete   Customers Customer Bank Accounts Id

items.docs: Delete a specified source for a given customer.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Bank Accounts Id Verify

items.docs: Verify a specified bank account for a given customer.

info.name: Cards
Stripe Get   Customers Customer Cards

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

items.docs: You can see a list of the cards belonging to a customer. Note that the 10 most recent sources are always available on the Customer object. If you need more than those 10, you can use this API method and the limit and starting_after parameters to page through additional cards.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Cards

items.docs:
When you create a new credit card, you must specify a customer or recipient on which to create it.

If the card’s owner has no default card, then the new card will become the default. However, if the owner already has a default, then it will not change. To change the default, you should update the customer to have a new default_source.

info.name: Stripe Get   Customers Customer Cards Id

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: You can always see the 10 most recent cards directly on a customer; this method lets you retrieve details about a specific card stored on the customer.

info.name: Stripe Post   Customers Customer Cards Id

items.docs: Update a specified source for a given customer.

info.name: Stripe Delete   Customers Customer Cards Id

items.docs: Delete a specified source for a given customer.
